mission.title: vault: Add Redis Secret backend
mission.description: Redis 6+ supports ACL-based users, making it possible to dynamically create and rotate credentials via Vault. This feature adds a Redis secret backend that allows operators to generate rotating Redis credentials instead of using hard-coded passwords. Requested by 39+ users.

steps.title: Check current Vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your Vault version supports the Redis database secrets engine:
```bash
vault version
vault secrets list
```
The Redis database secrets engine requires Vault 1.12+ and Redis 6.0+ (which introduced ACL support).

steps.title: Enable the database secrets engine
steps.description:
Enable the database secrets engine if not already enabled:
```bash
vault secrets enable database
```
This engine supports multiple database types including Redis.

steps.title: Configure the Redis database connection
steps.description:
Configure Vault to connect to your Redis instance:
```bash
vault write database/config/my-redis-db \
  plugin_name=redis-database-plugin \
  host=redis.example.com \
  port=6379 \
  tls=true \
  username=admin \
  password=admin-password \
  allowed_roles="my-redis-role"
```
Create a role that defines the credentials Vault will generate:
```bash
vault write database/roles/my-redis-role \
  db_name=my-redis-db \
  creation_statements='{"commands": ["ACL SETUSER {{username}} on >{{password}} ~* +@all"]}' \
  default_ttl=1h \
  max_ttl=24h
```

steps.title: Verify dynamic Redis credential generation
steps.description:
Generate dynamic credentials and test them:
```bash
# Generate credentials:
vault read database/creds/my-redis-role

# Test the generated credentials against Redis:
# The output will include a username and password that can be used
# to authenticate to Redis with the ACL permissions defined in the role.
```
Confirm that credentials are generated with the correct TTL and that they work for authenticating to Redis.

resolution.summary: Vault added a Redis database secrets engine that leverages Redis 6+ ACL support to dynamically create and rotate credentials. Operators can configure Vault with a Redis connection, define roles with ACL creation statements, and generate short-lived credentials that automatically expire, replacing hard-coded Redis passwords.
codeSnippets:
vault write database/config/my-redis-db \
  plugin_name=redis-database-plugin \
  host=redis.example.com \
  port=6379 \
  tls=true \
  username=admin \
  password=admin-password \
  allowed_roles="my-redis-role"

prerequisites.description: A running Vault server (1.12+) with admin access, and a Redis 6+ instance with ACL support enabled.
